batch_6_email_templates.note: Templates below are production-ready with [bracketed placeholders]. Fill from data/batch_6_leads.json once HANDS completes scrape. Assign signal per lead before populating. These templates are unchanged from the winning Batch 5 / Batch 1-4 copy — the model is proven.

ssl_pas_day0.subject_primary: [First name] — your [town] [business type] site is flagged 'Not Secure'
subject_rules: First name in subject if owner name is known — non-negotiable
No question marks — statement of fact
subject_examples: Mark — your Knaresborough plumbing site is flagged 'Not Secure'
Julie — your Skipton B&B website is flagged 'Not Secure'
David — your Ripon estate agency site isn't secure
Helen — your Boroughbridge joinery site is flagged 'Not Secure'
Sarah — your Settle builder website is flagged 'Not Secure'
ssl_pas_day0.body:
Hi [First name],

I was looking up [trade] in [town] this morning and noticed [specific URL] comes up with a 'Not Secure' warning in Chrome.

For visitors searching for a [trade] in [town], that warning appears before they've even read your page — it's the first thing they see. A lot will click away immediately.

The fix is quick and doesn't touch your existing site or content. Would it be worth a quick chat?

[Your name]
personalisation_required: First name (from enrichment — Companies House, website about page, Google Maps)
Specific URL (from SSL check — the exact URL flagged as HTTP, e.g. http://www.smithsplumbing.co.uk)
rules: Use the specific HTTP URL — not the domain alone. Shows you actually checked.
Town name in body opening line — builds local credibility
'Customers search for you online before calling' framing for trades
One pain point only — trust/warning. Do NOT mention mobile, ranking, or other issues in Day-0.
4-5 lines maximum — not a report
No attachments, no links

mobile_pas_day0.condition: ssl=false AND mobile clearly broken — confirm you tested it on a phone before sending
mobile_pas_day0.subject_primary: [First name] — [business name]: your [town] website doesn't work on mobile
subject_examples: Paul — Knaresborough Roofing: your website doesn't work on mobile
James — your Skipton joinery site has a mobile problem
mobile_pas_day0.body:
Hi [First name],

I was looking up [business name] on my phone and the site [specific issue: text too small to read / menu not working / images not loading / contact number not clickable].

Most people searching for a [trade] in [town] are on their phones. If the site doesn't work, they'll find the next result instead.

Worth a quick look? Happy to show you what the fix involves.

[Your name]
personalisation_required: Specific mobile issue (must be confirmed by actual phone test — not assumed)
rules: ONLY use if you have personally tested the site on a phone and confirmed the specific failure
Name the exact failure — 'text too small' or 'menu not working' builds credibility vs. 'mobile problem'
Do NOT use if site is also HTTP — use SSL-PAS instead (higher signal priority)
4-5 lines max

no_website_bab_day0.condition: No website at all AND 4★+ AND 20+ reviews (rural bar — lowered from 50 for Harrogate/Craven rural towns)
no_website_bab_day0.subject_primary: [First name] — [business name] has [X] Google reviews but no website to send people to
no_website_bab_day0.subject_fallback_no_owner_name: [Business name] — [town]: great Google reviews, no website
subject_examples: Mike — Boroughbridge Builders has 34 reviews but no website to send people to
Karen — your Grassington B&B has 47 reviews and nothing to back them up
no_website_bab_day0.body:
Hi [First name],

[Business name] has [X] reviews on Google and a [star rating] rating — that's a strong reputation.

Right now, anyone who finds you through Google can see the reviews but has nowhere to go next. No contact form, no booking page, no way to find your prices or availability. Most of them will call a competitor who has a site.

A simple one-page site with your services, area, and contact details would capture those searchers. I build them quickly and they're designed around what [trade] customers in [town] are actually looking for.

Worth five minutes?

[Your name]
personalisation_required: Review count (from Google Maps — exact number)
rules: Must have 4★+ AND 20+ reviews — no exceptions
Reference the exact review count — shows you actually looked at their Google listing
BAB structure: Before (great reviews, no site) → After (site captures searchers) → Bridge (I build them)
NOT a deficit framing — lead with their strength (reviews), then reveal the gap
No-website leads in rural towns often have owner-operated businesses — direct, warm tone

aida_day0.condition: 4.5★+ AND 100+ reviews only AND verifiable keyword gap — confirm in Google search before sending
aida_day0.subject_primary: [First name] — [business name] isn't showing up for '[trade] [town]' searches
aida_day0.subject_fallback_no_owner_name: [Business name] — [town]: not ranking for your main keyword
aida_day0.body:
Hi [First name],

[Business name] has [X] reviews and a [star rating] rating on Google — that's exceptional for [town].

But when someone searches '[trade] [town]' today, [business name] is [position/not appearing] in the results — [competitor name] and [competitor 2] are above you despite fewer reviews.

The gap is usually fixable with a few specific changes to the site. I checked this morning and can see exactly what's holding the ranking back.

Would it be worth a quick look together?

[Your name]
personalisation_required: Specific search query (verified by HANDS in Google today)
Competitor names appearing above (from actual search — do not guess)
rules: ONLY use if you have personally searched '[trade] [town]' today and confirmed the ranking gap
Name the specific competitors appearing above — builds verifiable credibility
4.5★+ AND 100+ reviews ONLY — this is the only angle that justifies the contrast claim
5% allocation — test only. Do not scale until conversion rate confirmed.
Avoid for sectors where PAS is proven (SSL leads) — PAS outperforms AIDA for HTTP sites

day3_bump_template.type: Thread reply — reply to Day-0 thread. Do NOT start new email.
day3_bump_template.imap_precondition: MANDATORY per lead before bump. If INTERESTED found: skip bump, use proposal flow (24h SLA). If NOT_INTERESTED: cancel sequence permanently. If UNSUBSCRIBE: suppress immediately.
day3_bump_template.linkedin_note: LINKEDIN MANDATORY DAY 3 — execute LinkedIn connection AND write profiles.json SAME SESSION as Day-3 bumps. Archive trigger Tue 2026-06-10 EOD fires unconditionally.

ssl_bump.body:
Hi [First name],

Just wanted to make sure this didn't get buried — the 'Not Secure' warning on [URL] is still live as of today.

Happy to give you 10 minutes to show you exactly what the fix looks like.

[Your name]

mobile_bump.body:
Hi [First name],

Just following up on the mobile issue with [business name] — still showing the [specific issue] when I tested this morning.

Would it be useful to talk through the fix? Shouldn't take long.

[Your name]

no_website_bump.body:
Hi [First name],

Just wanted to follow up — [business name] still has [X] reviews with nowhere to send people next.

A quick call would let me show you what a simple site would do for enquiries.

[Your name]

day7_new_angle_template.type: Standalone NEW email — NOT a thread reply. New subject line. Different pain point.

ssl_day7.subject: [First name] — the 'Not Secure' warning is also a Google ranking issue for [business name]
ssl_day7.body:
[First name],

Sent a note last week about the 'Not Secure' warning on [URL].

One more thing worth knowing: Google uses HTTPS as a ranking signal for local searches. HTTP sites don't just put visitors off — they rank lower when someone searches for '[trade] in [town]'. So the warning is costing [business name] both trust and visibility at the same time.

Worth sorting before a competitor moves above you. Happy to talk through what the fix looks like.

[Your name]

mobile_day7.subject: [First name] — [business name]: the mobile issue also affects your Google ranking
mobile_day7.body:
[First name],

Followed up last week about the mobile display issue on [URL].

Google's ranking algorithm uses mobile-friendliness as a signal for local searches. A site that doesn't work on phones ranks lower — so the issue you're seeing on mobile is also costing you visibility when someone searches '[trade] [town]'.

Happy to walk you through what both fixes look like together.

[Your name]

no_website_day7.subject: [First name] — every month without a site, a competitor in [town] moves further ahead
no_website_day7.body:
[First name],

Followed up last week about your Google reviews — [X] reviews with no site to back them up.

Searches for '[trade] in [town]' are going to competitors with sites right now. Each month without one is another month of those enquiries going elsewhere.

A simple site takes about a week to build. Happy to show you what it would look like for [business name].

[Your name]

day14_breakup_template.type: Standalone NEW email — NOT a thread reply. New subject line. 3 lines max.

ssl_day14.subject: [First name] — last note on the [town] site issue
ssl_day14.body:
[First name],

Sent a few notes about the 'Not Secure' warning on [URL]. No pressure — if the timing isn't right I completely understand.

If you ever want to revisit it, you know where I am.

[Your name]

mobile_day14.subject: [First name] — last note on the [business name] mobile issue
mobile_day14.body:
[First name],

Sent a couple of notes about the mobile display on [URL]. No bother if it's not the right time.

Happy to help if it ever moves up the list.

[Your name]

no_website_day14.subject: [First name] — last word on getting [business name] online
no_website_day14.body:
[First name],

Followed up a few times about a site for [business name]. No pressure — these things have their own timing.

If it becomes a priority later in the year, just drop me a line.

[Your name]
